name: Siam (State Flag, 1916–1917)

colors.symbolism: the caparisoned white elephant in royal regalia

description: A red field bearing a white elephant in full royal regalia (caparisoned, on a pedestal) at the centre — the state flag.
history: In 1916 Rama VI introduced a state flag showing the white elephant in royal regalia on the red field. It was used for state purposes until the trairanga tricolour was adopted in 1917.

features.role: caparisoned white elephant in royal regalia
